releases.notes:
Fixed: Question Detection When Question Text Is Last Line

Enhanced Pattern 2 (multiple-choice) detection to recognize questions where the question text itself (ending with '?') is the last line in the terminal buffer, rather than requiring an explicit '>' prompt. This fixes detection for Claude Code questions where numbered options are followed by question text like "Which would you prefer?" without the '>' prompt being captured yet. Added lastLineIsQuestion check and updated debug logging.

Added: Debug Logging for Question Detection

Added comprehensive diagnostic logging to help troubleshoot why Claude Code questions aren't being detected. Logs buffer size, last 5 lines, promptLine/lastLine values, and pattern matching results. Check SpecGofer output channel for debug information when questions appear in Claude Code terminal.

Improved: Question Detection

Enhanced autonomous question detection to handle Claude Code questions with decorative separator lines (Unicode box-drawing characters like ─━═). Added helper functions to skip separators and find actual prompts. Increased context window from 20 to 30 lines for better detection accuracy. Now supports 5 distinct question types: text input, multiple choice, yes/no, list selection, and general prompts.

Fix: Duplicate Spinner Output

This release fixes the duplicate spinner output issue in Claude Code terminal by implementing the missing setDimensions callback in the Pseudoterminal interface. Spinners now properly overwrite the same line instead of creating duplicate lines.
